title: [Suggestion] LiteDB option for new beta integrations
author: dethknite
body_snippet:
I have noticed the mysql/mssql integration coming in, and wanted to toss out the potential of a NoSQL db that works great and is tightly integrated in .NET.

https://github.com/mbdavid/LiteDB

* IMO, having less installs, running procs, and dependencies on external entities is always great.

iterations.description: To provide a lightweight, embedded NoSQL database option for new beta features and integrations, offering flexibility and an alternative to existing SQL-based connectors, specifically for new data models.
Verified: feature already implemented in codebase

notes:
AI triage (gemini): To provide a lightweight, embedded NoSQL database option for new beta features and integrations, offering flexibility and an alternative to existing SQL-based connectors, specifically for new data models.
Approach: Introduce LiteDB as an embedded NoSQL database by adding the LiteDB NuGet package and establishing a dedicated data access layer (e.g., context and unit of work) to manage LiteDB instances and operations. This will allow new beta integrations to leverage LiteDB's document-oriented capabilities without being constrained by the existing SQL-centric IDatabaseConnector.
